inhabitants.name: Sinbar
inhabitants.description: This is your master. He plays little role in the epic struggle other than to wish you well and send you into his testing grounds. He reasons that anyone unable to make it through the test is unready to be a Corsair, and does not let any leave the island without besting his construction.

locations.description: Start. Starting place for the game.
Flaming Passage. Later, flames such as this attack you, but these flame elementals are under the control of Sinbar; they part to grant you entrance.
Sinbar. After a brief conversation, he leads you to the mouth of his maze at Location 4.

Testing Ground Beginning. Sinbar wishes you well and takes his leave. Just to the south the teleporter field appears. It follows you through the dungeon, and one touch of its energy sends you (and it!) back to this starting point.
Rocks. Avoid them quickly.
Stationary Spears. Weave through without pausing.
Rising Spears. Avoid getting skewered on them.
Flaming Bolts. These are quite painful; however, it is possible to pass every one of the flaming bolt locations without getting hit.
Dead End. Do not come down here, for the teleporter will follow.
Plates and Flame Jets. Each pressure plate triggers some flame jets. Try to step on as few plates as possible and avoid the flames.
Rising Flame Jets. Time your moves just right to keep from getting damaged.
Razor Spikes. Quickly dodge to the side when these razors rise from the earth.
Plates and Spear Rows. Run over all three plates to drop the three rows of spears.
Vases. Smash them to get by.
Flame Elementals. Kill them or run past.
Claw and Razor Spikes. You can see the tips of the claw spikes — do not run over them. When the razor spikes rise, hurry and move to the side.
Piles of Gold. This gold is not worth collecting as the teleport field follows you into the dead end. If you are touched by the field, the gold disappears.
Invisible Bridge. The bridge becomes visible as you run over it. Take the fork to the left.
Saws. Time your passage to avoid being blocked or hit by the blades.
Arrow. This points to the switch at Location 21 which should be pulled.
Switches. Pull the switch referred to at Location 20. If you pull the correct switch, enjoy your startling flight past the blockade.
Single Spikes. Spikes slowly rise and lower from the ground; zip past them without getting stuck by one.
Openings. The ground opens and closes along this passage; dodge to the side of the pits and keep moving.
Rolling Earth Elementals. Later in the game you meet these creatures in combat. Now, however, they roll toward you in formations and must be dodged.
Bridge. The teleport field stops just past the bridge!
Reward. Sinbar has left two oils of invulnerability and 200 gold pieces for you.
Teleport Field. Step here for healing and to teleport back to Zaratan where you will live happily ever after — though not right away.

inhabitants.name: Aliya Al-Hazrad
inhabitants.description: Your sister tends to be a bit of an outcast, but this is largely by choice. She only feels comfortable in the Sorcerer’s Guild. She welcomes you at the gates when you first arrive at Zaratan. Later, you need to bring her before Haroon abi Wassab to sign the Qadi’s treaty. She also bears a very useful gift for you.

inhabitants.name: Babazar
inhabitants.description: Babazar is a rich merchant employed by both your family and the Wassabs. His daughter has taken very ill and he desperately needs an adventurer to head into the western desert to find medicinal berries. Babazar pays well for this simple task.

inhabitants.name: Haroon abi Wassab
inhabitants.description: The father of the family which rivals yours in Zaratan has mellowed with age. He favors conciliation over aggression, though his son and daughter-in-law do not. In order to convince him to sign the Qadi’s peace treaty, you need to bring your sister before him and swear with her not to harm him or his family.

inhabitants.name: Jessamin Al-Hazrad
inhabitants.description: Your mother was long ago a cleric and adventurer by the side of Zubin. However, many years ago she retired to start a family and enjoy the finer things in life. She is strong-willed and capable.

inhabitants.name: Latifah abi Wassab
inhabitants.description: Latifah, Mamoon’s wife, despises anything that has to do with the Al-Hazrads and would enjoy any ill which befell your family.

inhabitants.name: Mamoon abi Wassab
inhabitants.description: Location 22 or 24 — Latifah’s husband Mamoon hates every Al-Hazrad and would love to see your family hang from the town gates.

inhabitants.name: Merwani abi Wassab
inhabitants.description: The mother of the Wassabs, your family’s rivals, has no good will toward you and even calls upon her son to throw you into the street.

inhabitants.name: Muliban
inhabitants.description: Your family’s genie will be summoned to give an explanation of his actions. Unfortunately, there is no time to question him before you are thrown out of town.

inhabitants.name: Qadi
inhabitants.description: The political leader of Zaratan, and a friend of your father’s, the Qadi is obeyed by all citizens. He is known to be especially fond of the Caliph in his hopes for political gain. The Qadi has a peace treaty which you must retrieve and have your father and Haroon abi Wassab sign. Do not forget to bring the signed treaty back to him for ratification.

inhabitants.name: Sinbar
inhabitants.description: Your master appears here when you return from seeing the Pahari. He has grave words and two presents — a weakening word and a water blast shard.

inhabitants.name: Zubakon
inhabitants.description: The Weapons Master of Zaratan is a good friend. He spars with you, which gives you the chance to practice fighting. Also, after gaining enough experience to learn a new attack move, he teaches it to you in Bandar al-Sa’adat.

inhabitants.name: Zubin Al-Hazrad
inhabitants.description: Your father was once a great sha’ir, a mage who controls genies. He has retired from adventuring and now enjoys his profitable merchant business. Aiding him in everything he does is Muliban, the genie which he, his wife, his daughter, and his elder son all share control over. You need to have your father sign the peace treaty given to you by the Qadi. Zubin’s honor is well-respected, though some resent his power.

locations.description: Your Family’s Courtyard. Your father enjoys gardening here. Aliya takes you here after you first meet her at the gate.
Your House. Your mother works here.
Your Bed. Do not forget to check your dresser for a letter from your beloved.
Your Mother and Father’s Bed.
Your Brother’s House. Tarik is missing, but there is a letter in the dresser from him.

Babazar the Merchant’s House. Babazar desperately needs you to fetch some medicinal berries to save his daughter’s life. He gives you a sunfire shard to aid you, and only pays you if you demand a reward upon completion of the quest. His daughter lies in the northwest bed.

Your Aunt’s House. She is very happy to see you, and even has a wedding present in a basket in the northwest corner of the house, comprised of ten gems and one hundred gold pieces. Her first attempt at a present, as you shall see, was not exactly a success.
The Sorcerer’s Guild. A wizard attempts to impress you with his secret doors. However, after moving north through one and east through the other, he will not be so pleased.
The Sorcerer’s Guild. Another wizard has been perfecting a spell to restore broken objects. Help her with her work!
The Sorcerer’s Guild. These rooms are only accessible via secret doors which appear after speaking to the wizard at Location 8. Just walk right into the wall.
The Barracks. The captain of the guard is happy to see you, and warns you of the dangers to the west of town.
The Barracks. These are the sleeping quarters for the guards.
The Inn. The rather thick-headed innkeeper works here.
The Inn. Staying in this room is a woman who has come to study Muliban, your family’s genie. She holds some words of warning about geniekind.
The Inn. This guest would rather be left alone.
The Inn. In this room is a man bent on undertaking a perilous expedition. He talks himself out of it, but clues you in on upcoming dangers.
The Weapons Master. Zubakon has his shop here, and is always glad to teach new sword moves when you are experienced enough, spar with you, or tell you where your sword might be useful.

The Qadi. The ruler of Zaratan dispenses justice from this building. He has a treaty for your father and Haroon abi Wassab to sign. After it has been signed, he also needs to be given the message from the Pahari.
The Temple. The priest holds words of advice, and is always pleased to accept any donations.

The Qadi’s House. While you are not allowed here, the guard believes you if you tell him you have permission — people fear your family with its control of Muliban. Inside, there is little of value, but do check the dresser for any notes.
The Shopkeeper’s House.
Haroon and Merwani abi Wassab's House. You are unwelcome here, but must enter with the Qadi's treaty for Haroon to sign.
Poor Man’s House. This resident has hit hard times and needs money to fix his house. Whether or not you decide to help him, come back after exploring the desert to see how he is making out.
Mamoon and Latifah's House.
The Potion Shop. Your friend Mullad runs this store, an inexpensive place to purchase potions of healing.
Under Construction. This building is currently being renovated.
Your Sister's House. After meeting you at the gate, Aliya is found here. Bring her with you to sign the peace treaty. On your first visit, she gives you a sling of seeking.
The Store Room.
The Qadi’s Courtyard. This is where Haroon abi Wassab meets you and your sister to sign the treaty. It is also the location of the accusation of your family and the summoning of Muliban.

The Main Gate. After the magical storm abates and you return from the oasis, your mother meets you here with the terrible news of the havoc Muliban has wrought. Later, when returning after rescuing the Caliph, the Qadi meets you here to dampen the Caliph’s gratitude.
The Direction of the Western Desert, Sorcerer’s Tower, and oasis. All of these places are reached by heading west.
The Wassab’s Courtyard. Latifah stalks this area.
Sinbar. Your master appears here after you return from seeing the Pahari. He gives grave words and two presents: a weakening word and a water blast shard.
The Oasis. This is the home of the pahari, a mermaid. She is instrumental in your quest to leave the Sorcerer’s Isle. Also found at the oasis are the purple berries for the merchant
The Purple Berries. Gather these and bring them to Babazar in Zaratan.
The Submerged Bridge. After gaining permission from the pahari, you can cross the submerged bridge.
The Healing Location. This is detailed above. Once entered, all of your hit points are restored.

The Shipwrecked Caliph. After the Wassab's ship is destroyed by Muliban, the Caliph can be found trying to evade wild debbi under a palm tree on the western shore. He will demand to be taken to Zaratan after you find a treasure.
The Caliph's Treasure. A small chest partially concealed behind a broken barrel holds the Caliph's treasure: a magical locket of the princess that lets the bearer know if she is alive.
The Sorcerer's Tower. This is the entrance to the Sorcerer Farid's Tower.

The Sea Turtle. When the Pahari tells you to journey to the southwestern peninsula, and imparts to you the magic words, a sea turtle appears by the shore. In order to ride on the back of the Pahari’s friend, just step on board. It swims to the Reef of the Dead.
Rowboat. When coming to the desert from the ship, your rowboat lands here.
Sea Turtle. This is where the pahari’s sea turtle friend drops you off. It also carries you back to Zaratan should you need to leave the island for recuperation.
Small Key. A chest behind the plants holds a key, needed in the hold of the rotting ship, as well as forty gold pieces.
Large Key. The key, needed in the ship’s hold, is in a chest hidden in the bushes along with sixty gold pieces.
Green Key. The third key needed in the rotting hold is found after killing a ghoul.
Zombies. There are zombies hiding behind mirage spells at these locations.
Ghouls. There are ghouls hiding behind mirage spells at these locations.
Plank. The ship can be boarded from the plank.
The Rotting Ship. A pirate armed with a bow guards the ship.
The Stairway to the Ship’s Hold.
Gold. These locations are all piles of real gold pieces.
The Cobweb Captain. A statue of the ship’s captain stands here deteriorating with its stone hand firmly gripping the ship’s rudder.
Water Elementals. These miniature counterparts of their larger cousins spring onto the shore for a surprise attack.
The Stairs to the Surface.
The First Room. It is empty.
The First Door. The door needs the small key, found on the island at Location 2.
The Second Room. Ghouls serve as a final round of guards here.
The Second Door. This door requires two keys: the large key and the green key.
The Cleric's Room. Sashana works magic here and fires spells immediately. Behind her on the floor are an oil of air elemental invulnerability and a potion of healing.
Sashana’s Chest. The chest contains 250 gold pieces and five gems.
The Magic Portal. Approaching the far wall activates the magic portal. Entering it pulls you out of the Prime Material Plane and onto the Enchanted Platform.
Note. A fruitless note from Sashana’s mentor lies ignored on the floor.
The Captain. From here you lead the magical craft onwards. If you know the name of an island, the captain can take you there.
The Rowboat. After anchoring at your destination, board the rowboat to go ashore.
Stairway to the Hold.
Gangplank Opening. Pirates you may encounter board your ship from this point.
Entrance.
The Teleport Trap. There is no avoiding this invisible wall.
Stairway to Level Two.
Stairway from the Acid Dungeon. You may not reenter this portal.
Entrance Hall. Walk onto the carved face to proceed past the door at Location 3. Farid asks why you entered his tower; there is no correct answer. A barrel northwest holds twelve gems.
Blue Circles. Light after speaking with Farid at Location 1. Step on each lit circle until the door at Location 3 opens.
Metal Door. Opened using the circles at Location 2.
Floor Switch. Raises a platform at Location 5.
Acid Trough. Passable only after pulling the switch at Location 4. Acid blobs hide in the trough and attack.
Floor Switch. Lowers spikes at Location 9.
Row of Spikes. Lowered with the floor switch at Location 10.
Acid Blobs. Acid puddles may contain blobs that attack if disturbed.
Row of Spikes. Lowered with the floor switch at Location 6.
Floor Switch. Lowers spikes at Location 7.
Rat Attack. Giant Zaratan rats attack.
Stone Face. Step on it and introduce yourself to Farid. A barrel in the southeast corner holds eighty gold pieces.
Wall Switch. Activates the secret door at Location 14.
Secret Door. After pulling Location 13's switch, this wall rhythmically bulges and can be walked through to Location 15.
Secret Room. Holds the switch to lower spears at Location 17.
Treasure Chest. Contains twelve gold pieces and four potions of healing.
Row of Spears. Lowered with the switch at Location 15.
Stone Face. Farid challenges you to explain why you are worthy of an audience. Honor of family is important. Once satisfied, he raises the platform at Location 19.
Floating Platform. Ride it across the acid; if you stay too long, it returns.
Acid Blob Attack. Acid blobs jump from the acid behind you and attack.
Small Rune Room. One rune is blue and the rest red. Red shocks you. Step on blue runes as they light until none remain; then the wall switch at Location 22 becomes usable.
Wall Switch. After completing Location 21's runes, pull this to turn all runes blue and help activate the secret door at Location 25. Both switches at 22 and 24 are needed.
Rhythmic Spears. Carefully pass when retracted or barely visible.
Wall Switch and Treasure Chest. Chest contains ten gems and three potions of extra-healing. Pulling the switch teleports you back across the spears and helps activate Location 25.
Secret Door. Switches at Locations 22 and 24 make it pulsate; then it can be entered.
Vases. Smashing one reveals a potion of healing.
Secret Door. Floor runes point to the passage wall; walk east at the arrow point to pass through.
Treasure Chest. Holds two potions of healing and one potion of extra-healing.
Stone Face. Farid warns you of the acid.
Wall Switch. Pull this, then Location 31's switch, to raise the platform at Location 32.
Wall Switch. Pull after Location 30 to raise the platform at Location 32.
Floating Platform. Raised by switches at 30 and 31; carries you to the small island at Location 33.

Small Island. Walk to the center to speak with the Acid King. Five mistakes in the tests cause platforms 34 or 36 to sink into acid. First arrival raises Location 34 and sinks the platform behind you. After solving Location 35, Location 34 sinks and Location 36 rises. After Locations 37 and 38, the Acid King teleports you to Location 40.
Floating Platform. Rises after first stepping in Location 33's center; sinks after returning from Location 35.

Stone Face. Farid tests honesty and observation. Run over lit blue runes until none light, then answer questions about which rune did not light, which rune besides northeast lit twice, and what the rune stands for. Failures require retrying from the island; five failures are fatal.
Floating Platform. Rises after completing Location 35 and returning to Location 33; sinks after returning from Locations 37 and 38.
Spike Puzzle. Pull switches A-E to create a path through spikes. Easiest combination: A, B, D. Farid and his wife can be heard speaking through the stone face.

Invisible Guardian. After Location 37, a guardian questions whether you intend to harm Farid. Keep your story consistent to convince her. Changing your story or threatening Farid forces a retry; five failures are fatal.
The Acid King. After Locations 35, 37, and 38, he rises from the acid and teleports you to the northern edge of the acid pool.
Treasure Chest. Contains two potions of extra-healing, five gems, and one hundred gold pieces.
Vase.
Spider Attack. Huge spiders attack in the dungeon hall.

Floating Platform Room. Filled with platforms that travel to destinations. Four islands are Locations 44-47. Route: A-B-C-D-44-D-C-E-45-E-F-G-H-I-J-K-L-46-L-K-M-N-O-P-47-P-O-N-M-K-J-Q. Platform Q rises when the switch at Location 46 is pulled.
Island One. Chest holds five gems, fifty gold pieces, and one potion of extra-healing.
Island Two. Chest holds seventy-five gold pieces and two potions of extra-healing.
Island Three. Pull the floor switch to raise platform Q at Location 43.
Island Four. Chest holds eighty gold pieces and one potion of extra-healing.
Stone Face. Farid asks what type of man would try to steal a woman's heart from her husband. Speaking activates the maze at Location 49.

The Maze. Maze of lights activated by Location 48. Step only on safe runes; safe adjacent runes briefly illuminate. First goal is the switch at Location 51. After pulling it, the maze changes and the goal becomes the southwesternmost corner. Teleporters are hidden on blue runes. Red runes shock and teleport you back to the beginning.
Vase. Smash it for a potion of extra-healing and two gems.
Wall Switch. Sets Location 49's maze to its second pattern and fills the acid trough at Location 53 with stone.
Treasure Chest. Contains a potion of extra-healing.
Acid Trough. Impassable until the switch at Location 49 is pulled.
Floating Platform. Carries you across the acid.
Stone Face. Doryna Al'Mutan asks how you feel about the Sorcerer wanting her to remain in the tower.
Hallway Spears. Run over fully retracted or barely visible spears, or run along the left wall.
Rising Platform Room. Pull switches in order to raise platforms across the acid. Run to each last platform. Order: A-B-C-D-B-E-F-G-H-I. Secret room lies southeast.
Secret Acid Room. Accessible only after pulling switch I at Location 57. A platform north of switch H carries you through the secret door. Chest contains four potions of extra-healing.
Stone Face. Farid forces you to promise not to seek the Pahari again.
The Acid King. Approaching the acid pool makes him form a face; he lets you ask one question.
Stairway to Sorcerer's Tower Level One.
Chest. Holds 350 gold pieces and a sunscorch shard.
Stairway Down to Level One.
The Sorcerer and His Wife. They have information which will further your quest. Farid has a mirror for you, and if you return with the Gilded Dove, Doryna has a weakening word.
Stairway to the Sorcerer’s Private Chambers. These are offlimits and not accessible.

inhabitants.name: Guards
inhabitants.description: The guards of Bandar al-Sa’adat are not friendly. If you get in trouble in town, one of them is dispatched to track you down. The others catch you if you approach them. If you are caught twice, you will be imprisoned and your quest will end.

inhabitants.name: Zubakon
inhabitants.description: The Weapons Master from Zaratan has moved his shop to Bandar al-Sa’adat. He will still teach you new sword moves when you have gained enough experience to learn, and will spar with you to practice those moves.

locations.description: The Rowboat. Return to the ship by stepping on the rowboat.
The Main Gates. The guards challenge your entrance to the city, but must let you in as you are of noble birth.

Trade Office and Money Changer. You may exchange your gold for gems here or vice versa. One gem is given for eleven gold pieces and ten gold pieces is given for one gem. After being sent by Sumia to the Trade Office, ask about the trade business. When invited, step behind the counter to privately speak with him.
Barracks.
For Rent. Former scribe's shop; the scribe was arrested for forging the vizier's seal.
Guard.
Supernatural Emporium. The shopkeeper has many magical items for sale. You can purchase both potions and shards from him. After Doryna al'Mutan (from the Sorcerer's Tower).
Mess Hall.
Traveler's Rest. The inn was closed down as the city became more restrictive. People still sit inside and talk, but there is no business. The most important person here is Qutlum ibn Tasheed.
Ha-Kim's Heating. Coal is for sale here, though you don't need it.
Fountain.
Reptilian Desires. This shop has the Wisest Snake for sale.
Desert Salve-ation. Healer heals wounds for a fee; no charity.
Sand's Point Gambling Club. Gambling house. Bet fifty to 500 gold pieces to win one hundred to 1000. Guess a hidden number from 1 to 100 in six guesses; dealer tells higher/lower and never cheats.
Private Residence. Enterable, but staying in others' houses too often summons a guard.
Weapons Master. Zubakon has moved here from Zaratan; he can train new sword moves and spar.
Ingrid’s Shop of Wonders. While her honey bread does taste good, it does nothing to aid in your quest.
Guard. The guard on the western side of the gate is the one who chases you if alerted.
Omar the Baker. Omar sells his wares here, and he also has the key to the old dungeon.
The Carpet Vendor.
The Meat Vendor.
The Fish Vendor.
Guard. The guard does not let you travel into the rest of the city as travel papers are required.
Beggar. This beggar demands gold; if he doesn’t get it he becomes verbally abusive. Give him ten gold pieces for a fun experience.

Con Man. A chest lies in the open here. If you touch it, a thief jumps out from behind the bushes and accuses you of stealing. He will, however, not inform the guards if you pay him fifty gold pieces. He can be bargained down to twenty-five, but if you do anything else other than pay him, he runs away and calls the guards.
The Palace Gate. This gate leads to the courtyard in front of the palace.
Courtyard.
Dissenter. He advises caution when dealing with the guards.
Pot Vendor.
Entrance to the Town.
Palace Guards. These guards are required to let you by, as you are a noble.
Entrance to the Palace.
Dungeon Grate. It is possible to speak with a prisoner through this grate.

inhabitants.name: Caliph
inhabitants.description: The Caliph sits in the throne room and will not speak to you until an audience is granted. He is becoming a more uncaring and ruthless dictator every day. Even though you saved his life in the deserts near Zaratan, if you anger him too greatly, he will throw you into a dungeon cell to rot. An audience with the Caliph is achieved by speaking with the vizier. If you use tact, you are able to convince the Caliph to allow you one visit to see your father. Guards The guards, found throughout the palace, generally ignore you. However, if you are escorting a prisoner out of the palace, found in an area off limits to you, or harming a noble, they will throw you into the dungeon.

inhabitants.name: Sabahar
inhabitants.description: The head guard stands at the top of the dungeon steps. He does not let you pass until the Caliph has granted permission. Even then, to descend the steps, Sabahar demands a bribe. After you return from the dungeon and see your father’s cell, Sabahar will accept payment to upgrade your father to a cleaner cell.

inhabitants.name: Vizier
inhabitants.description: Location 2 or 6 — The vizier, high servant to the Caliph, greets you on your first visit. After that he is found in the antechamber of the throne room working with his assistants. He is a dour man and does not take kindly to humor or plotting against the Caliph. Through the vizier, you are able to earn an audience with his eminence.

locations.description: Palace Gate. Leave here to exit back into the town.

Welcoming Party. The vizier and his assistant welcome you to the palace and give you an opportunity to request an audience with the Caliph. If you do indeed ask to see his eminence, the vizier has his assistant escort you to a waiting room at Location 3.
Waiting Room. The escort brings you to this room to wait for the palace gong to sound, which signals your audience.

Throne Room. If you have an audience and the gong has rung, the Caliph speaks to you. Otherwise, you are asked to leave. The nobles standing in this room are not particularly friendly as your family bears a rather negative reputation.
Stairs to the New Dungeon. These steps are guarded by Sabahar.
Vizier’s Antechamber. After greeting you in the southern hall, the vizier is found here working with his assistants.

Servant with the Brotherhood of Freedom. Once the Caliph will no longer grant an audience with you, this servant (there are two, whichever one you walk near first) approaches to tell you to see Sumia; heed this advice.

The Head Chef. Sumia, member of the Brotherhood of Freedom. First, she sends you to speak with the trade officer in the town to retrieve a key to the dungeon’s secret door. Then, she directs you to the entrance and tells you all she knows about the old dungeon.
Empty Room with Secret Door to the Old Dungeon. With the key from Omar in the town, and after Sumia tells you where the door is, this wall will open to a stairway leading to the old dungeon.
Patrolling Guard. If you are breaking the law, be careful to avoid this guard as he patrols around the outside of the vizier’s antechambe, at Location 6.
Dining Area.
Meditation Room.
Vizier’s Chambers, Work Room. Off-limits to you.
Vizier's Chambers, Rest Room. There are many books lying about this room with information concerning events in the lands. The scroll found in the bureau details the vizier's troubles with the Caliph.
Locked Door.
Patrolling Guard. Be careful coming from Locations 14, 17, and 19. If you get too near this guard, or if he faces you, you will spend the rest of your days in the dungeon.
Tea Cart. Push this cart past the guards at Location 17A to distract them — enabling you to dash south into the harem.
The Harem. Off-limits to you.
The Caliph’s Private Chambers. Off-limits to you.
Caliph’s Chambers. Off-limits to you.
Caliph’s Chambers. Off-limits to you.
Secret Room. Off-limits to you.
Servant.

Obdel’s Room. Requesting an audience with you. He speaks of who is behind the plot which engulfed your family, and assures you that proof of what he says can be found in the Caliph’s secret room. When you agree to his plan, Obdel gives you a ring which will teleport you into the closed areas of the palace.
Nobles’ Rooms.
Kitchen Rooms.
Royal Banquet Hall.
Guard Rooms. Do not enter if you are breaking the law or you will be imprisoned.
Festival Hall.

inhabitants.name: Shubakan
inhabitants.description: This man holds the dubious honor of being the only justly imprisoned person in the dungeon. He is a forger who was caught with copies of the vizier’s official seal. He had been hired by an unknown noble for the job; now the forger’s store rests empty in Bandar al-Sa’adat.

inhabitants.name: Zubin al-Hazrad
inhabitants.description: When you first enter the dungeon, a guard will escort you to your father. He is clearly in ill health, although a healing potion will help him some. He gives you a +1 ring of protection if you tell him how the rest of the family is faring.

locations.description: Stairs up to the Palace. When you descend the stairs, a guard immediately escorts you to your father.

Zubin's Cell. Here you find your father, although he is in very ill health due to the terrible condition of this section of the dungeon. You can earn a +1 ring of protection from him. If you did not pay Sabahar to upgrade your father, Zubin is still found here.
Shubakan. The forger is wasting away in this cell and will gladly talk to you of his crimes.

Aliya’s Cell. Your sister is slowly going mad here — her free spirit is struggling with the confinement. Make sure that a miniature copper automaton does not catch you in front of her cell, or the alarm will end your adventure.

Jessamin’s Cell. Your mother is rather angry with the Caliph but is surviving with no trouble. Make sure that a miniature copper automaton does not catch you in front of her cell, or the alarm will end your adventure.
Prisoner. Nearly all the prisoners will want to tell you their story.
Empty Cell. These serve well as temporary hiding places as the automatons pass.
Patrolling Human Guard. This is the sole human who patrols in the dungeon; the others stay at stationary posts. He will imprison you on sight.
Miniature Copper Automaton. This hunk of metal cannot sense very far away, but if you get too close, it sets off the alarm.
Better Cell. If you pay Sabahar to move your father to a better cell, this will be it.
Best Cell. If you pay Sabahar to move your father to the best cell, this will be it.
Guard Rooms. Entering requires an immediate bribe or you are arrested.
Passage to the Old Dungeon.
Secret Door. This door can only be opened with the key found in the Old Dungeon.

inhabitants.name: Rashidin
inhabitants.description: Location(s) 16 — This mad hermit is the guardian of the great library. After satisfying the statues at Location 13, he arrives to learn what you desire. With conversation, he can reveal that you need the name of the Genie Lords' island. Once he invites you to follow him into the library, the passageway to the second level is revealed. He does not stay still and flies between all locations marked 16.

locations.description: Your Rowboat. Step here to return to the ship.
Empty Room.
Scroll Room. Hidden in the rubble is a scroll named the "Stone Heart Scroll." Pick it up; it is needed for the guardians at Location 13.

Blocked Room. Move the blockade from the doors to enter and leave. There is a pile of seventy-five gold pieces in the rubble. The "Libraries Scroll" is near the gold, though it contains no useful information.
Nearly Empty Room. A pile of eighty-four gold pieces lies on the floor.
Book Room. A ghoul walks between this room and the hallway to the south. The "Tribute Book" is on the floor; memorizing it allows easier passage by the statues at Location 13.
Hallway. A ghoul wanders between this hall and the room to the north. There are twenty-three gold pieces in the hall.
Scroll Room. A ghoul guards a scroll called "Enemies." Smashing a barrel reveals an oil of earth elemental invulnerability. The "Enemies" scroll contains no useful information.
Basket. The basket holds eighty-nine gold pieces.
Basket. This basket contains nine gems and a water blast shard.
Living Furniture. Enchanted furnishings rush to attack intruders when disturbed. After defeating them, nineteen gems and an oil of fire elemental invulnerability can be recovered.
Ghuls. These lanterns mark the haunt of the ghuls.

Stone Guardians. Two statues must be satisfied before they lower the flames to the north. Guardian 13A requires gold or reading the "Tribute Book" section from Location 6. Guardian 13B requires feeding it the "Stone Heart Scroll." All other scrolls are considered blasphemous. Satisfying both guardians and walking north past the retracted flames summons Rashidin.
Living Chest. Holds riches but must be outwitted. If angered, it summons ghuls. Forgo its gold and gems; convince it to open farther, then quickly grab the moonstone shard.
Treasure. A pile of seventy-five gold pieces and a pile of 118 gold pieces lie here.
Rashidin. The mad hermit can be found at one of these locations, or flying on his carpet between them, after being summoned at Location 13. A marks primary locations; B marks other locations.
Fake Tomb. After Rashidin invites you into the library, the tomb slides aside, revealing a stairway down to level two.
Undead Room. A ghoul guards a pile of fifty-four gold pieces.
Treasure. Thirty gold pieces lie here.

inhabitants.name: Rashidin
inhabitants.description: Throughout the dungeon — Rashidin randomly flies about and may seek you out to provide information. His madness or cleverness prevents him from giving exactly what you ask for. Do not anger him while navigating the tricks and traps guarding the library.

locations.description: Stairway to Level One.
Vase. Smashed vases can be walked over.
Row of Spikes. You can only walk over these when fully retracted.
Rune, First Set. All of these runes must be darkened to create a passage through the spike rows at Location 14.
Ghul Lair. Ghuls live among the stone pillars circling this area.
Moving Statues. Be quick to avoid collision damage from these magical statues.
Bridge and Switch. A switch and retracted bridge sit here. Pulling the switch extends the bridge over the chasm.

Revolving Spikes. Two spike rows are visible when approached, limiting direction. If approaching from the south, you can only travel west. To open the way north, walk over Location 8A to reset the spikes, then go around west and approach from Location 8B.
Vase. A flame elemental hides behind this vase.
Six Curious Statues.
Room of Vases. Smash all vases to find one potion of giant strength, one oil of fire elemental invulnerability, twenty-three gems, and one hundred gold pieces.
Flaming Bridge. This narrow bridge becomes engulfed in flames after crossing.
Spike Maze. Wind through the spikes and back off the island over one of the stone bridges. The chest holds three potions of extra-healing and one potion of giant strength.
Spike Rows. A passage is forged through each row by walking over the runes at Location 4. The chest holds a sunfire shard, ten gems, and 200 gold pieces.
Bridge and Switch. This bridge catches fire after walking on it — move quickly.
Runes and Magic Gate. Step on the lit rune to drop the force field. If trying to pass the field again later, step on a lit rune and avoid unlit runes.
Rune, Second Set. Darken this rune to activate the puzzle at Location 20.
Book. Holds a clue to passing the puzzle at Location 20.
Vase. A mysterious rogue hides behind this vase and attacks without a word.

Rune and Statue Puzzle. Statues begin to move and chase you. Throwing the switch stops them briefly. To form the bridge of bones, step on the five lit runes. Faulty steps on other runes do not harm progress.
Bridge of Bones. After completing the puzzle at Location 20, a bridge of skeletons forms to allow passage.
The Magic Carpet. Step on the carpet to ride it. It remains on this level but can take you to hidden treasures and the staircase down. To get off, fly over a landing rune at Location 24.
Book. Used to cross the abyss. Read it aloud and walk off the north edge; a twinkling bridge appears.
Landing Rune. While flying on the carpet, hover over one of these runes to land.
Chest. Contains 250 gold pieces and ten gems.
Scroll. A scroll left by Farid al-Mutan advises you to find all lit runes.
Scroll. Tells you the next direction to take is east.
Scroll. Farid explains that you must fly over a landing rune to get off the carpet.
Healing Potion. A potion of healing lies on the ground.
Twinkling Passage. Lit by the book at Location 23. Keep trying to walk off each lit area; the bridge continues to appear.
Chest. Holds 350 gold pieces.

inhabitants.name: Rashidin
inhabitants.description: Throughout the Library — Once again the hermit can be found flitting about the dungeon level. Sometimes he stops to chat, and other times he continues about his business. It is possible to learn the name of the Genie Lords' Island through Rashidin. After leaving him the mirror in chests at Location 4 or 8, he eventually flies by and takes it. Once he has the gift, you can learn the island's name by speaking with him.

inhabitants.name: Ogrima
inhabitants.description: Two ogrima companions of Rashidin wander this area and are hostile to library visitors.

locations.description: Stairway to Level Two.
Scroll. Many scrolls throughout the library contain information or thoughts; some may be useful.
Smashable Vase.
Chest. The "Casket of Worldly Cares." If you have the mirror, drop it here as a gift for the hermit.
Reading Room. Pirates are looting this room. After dispatching them, you can find twelve gems, 135 gold pieces, ninety-seven gold pieces, and a scroll with a startling prediction.
Secret Passage. Walk through this wall to reach the secret area of the library.
Chest. Holds a +2 ring of protection.
Chest. The "Casket of Eternal Hopes." If you have the mirror, drop it here as a gift for the hermit.
Ogrima. Two ogrima companions of Rashidin wander here and attack library visitors.
Final Scroll. Holds the name of the Genie Lords' island. Found by smashing the vase in front of it.
Farid's Scroll. The last scroll left by Farid al-Mutan.
Smashable Vase. Contains a fire arrow shard.
Chest. Contains a sundazzle shard.

inhabitants.name: Azrail Tajrish
inhabitants.description: We djinn elected to use a human to guard the gate; since all power was divested from us on Jaza’ir Jiza by Mizra Gubishbuskin, it would be a waste of time for the unfortunate djinni posted to that duty. Therefore, Azrail the sha’ir incessantly floats about the island pretending that his position holds importance. Perhaps for him it does, as there is no better place to learn of geniekind on the Prime Material Plane than Jaza’ir Jiza. After tracking him down, Azrail can be useful in discovering a quicker way past Fuwwah than by completing her four tasks. In order to gain this information, alternate speaking between Azrail and Zuqalamushayt until they finally divulge the method.

inhabitants.name: Fuwwah ash Ghazha
inhabitants.description: This efreet guards the gate leading to the Genie Lords. She uses this responsibility as an opportunity to belittle others and to stroke her rather large ego. There are two methods of gaining access to the Lords through her. The first is to journey for the items which she requests, four in all, and bring each one back to her. The second is to speak with Zuqalamushayt and Azrail and discover the one item which could be used to bypass Fuwwah. While undertaking the first option, do not give up when speaking to her. If you grovel in an incorrect way, try groveling again, or giving praise. She has mood swings and will not always deign to give you another task. However, with persistence, she can be led to giving out quests until you have achieved all four of them. It is important to journey to Bandar al-Sa’adat for each of these missions.

locations.description: Your Rowboat. Step here to travel back to the ship. Visit with the Djinni Sisters.
Fists of Sand. The sand fist spell is permanently placed throughout the island, requiring you to travel to the far south in order to gain access to Fuwwah. Visit with the Marid.

Fuwwah ash Ghazha. The efreeti guardian floats at her post, keeping all visitors from bothering the Genie Lords. She must be outwitted to secure passage beyond her. See the Dao to learn of her desire to trade Shahar Izad for a present.

Zuqalamushayt. The dao guardian’s post is here, though he often wanders away from it in boredom. To summon him, step on the carpet, causing a gong to strike, and wait for his arrival. If a gong does not strike, return later. See the Djinn until they give you a sky gem for the Dao.

Azrail Tajrish. The human sha’ir who serves the djinni is rarely found in one place for long. While he will always return to his small, sand fist enclosed area, he can also be found flitting about the island. Give the gem to the Dao.

Giant Chessboard. Chessboard Portal to the Lords. To invoke the power of the board, stand one square in from a break in the vases surrounding the board, and then walk in the pattern of teleportation. Walk in five squares, turn left and walk three squares, and then turn left again and walk one square — be sure to walk with straight steps. You will know that the path is correct as long as each new square lights with a color. If you make a mistake, move back to the outside and try again. 6A Path to the Noble Marid. Each step colors green. 6B Path to the Noble Djinni. Each step colors purple. 6C Path to the Noble Efreet. Each step colors red. 6D Path to the Noble Dao. Each step colors blue. Get Shahar Izad from her cell in the southeast of the Dao’s region.
Secret Way. Try here for a quick entrance. Lead her to Jaza’ir Jiza where she will rush to see the Marid.
See the Marid to gain a way of passing over the Noble Efreeti’s chessboard and to free Shahar Izad.
See the Noble Efreeti. Use the Marid’s gift to pass through the chessboard flames and then gain from him the needed information to continue your quest.
Your Rowboat. Step here to travel back to the ship.
Treasure Chest. It holds a sunscorch shard, a potion of healing, ten gems, and one hundred gold pieces.
Treasure Chest. It contains a sunfire shard, a potion of healing, and 300 gold pieces.
The Final Treasure Chest. It contains the Bottle of Emptiness or the Singing Scimitar, if you are embarking on the Efreeti path.

inhabitants.name: Pangar-Bek
inhabitants.description: This desert cyclops deeply desires to be free. Captured and thrown into the dungeon for no reason other than his huge eye, he wants nothing but to escape from humans and their cities. You will have a choice upon encountering him. If you believe that he will indeed leave peacefully upon freedom, you can agree to lead him out of the dungeon. Otherwise, draw your sword and be prepared to fight a very angry desert cyclops. If you do decide to free him, see "The Freeing of Pangar-Bek" on page 73.

locations.description: Stairway to the Caliph’s Kitchen. You may come and go from the dungeon at will until you have finally reached your father for the second time.
Blocked Passage. The Brotherhood of Freedom’s collapsed tunnel lies down this hall. There is no way through it.
Old Room. Moving the blocks reveals a journal entry at Location 3B from a former explorer and a key at Location 3A, needed later for a secret door at Location 6.
Old Hall. Blocks of movable stone lie in the way.
Old Guard Chamber. Spiders live here.
Secret Door. This door needs the key found at Location 3A.
First Cell. The lever here can be found using the method described in the journal entry at Location 9.
Hallway. The rock in the center of the hall needs to be used on the pressure plates at both the western and eastern ends for the switches in the rooms, and Locations 11 and 10, respectively.

Second Cell, the Home of Pangar-Bek. The desert cyclops lives here and will not let you search his cell until you have either lead him to freedom or killed him. If you do decide to free him, see "The Freeing of Pangar-Bek" on page 73 for more information. The journal entry found in this cell details how to find the six switches at Locations 7, 9, 10, 11, 12, and 13, to open the door at Location 14. Move the rock found in this cell onto the pressure plate before attempting the switch.

Third Cell. Zombies are trapped in this cell. After dispatching them, the switch can be found and pulled. First, however, make sure to pull the rock in the hallway at Location 8 all the way down to cover the pressure plate outside the cell door.
Fourth Cell. Spiders angrily live in this cell. The switch in this room needs the rock in the hallway at Location 8 to be moved over the plate outside the cell door.
Fifth Cell. Opening the door reveals bloodthirsty ghouls. The room contains a stone block which is used to cover the switch plate.
Sixth Cell. The final cell holds acid blobs and a pressure plate with its own stone to be placed over it.
To open this door, pull every switch at Locations 7, 9, 10, 11,12, and 13 (see the journal entry at Location 9 for details).

Old Dining Hall. Earth elementals reign in this room. The wall switch on the north wall is revealed by moving both the stone table and the rock onto the two pressure plates in the southern end of the room and then hitting the wall as before. If only one plate is covered, spikes will rise from the floor and impale you. The door at Location 17 is opened by this switch.
Switches A-H. Each switch opens one of the cells at Locations 19-26. A opens 19, B opens 23, C opens 20, D opens 24, E opens 21, F opens 25, G opens 22, and H opens 26.
Locked Door. This door is opened with the switch at Location 15.
Hallway. A mad sorcerer lives here and has no qualms about showing you his spells.
Cell One. Fire elementals roam this hall, guarding a chest with 300 gold pieces.
Cell Two. Ghouls guard a treasure chest with 150 gold pieces.
Cell Three. A great ghul guards a treasure chest with 250 gold pieces.
Cell Four. Ghouls guard a treasure chest with one hundred gold pieces.
Cell Five. Acid blobs rest in this cell.
Cell Six. A massive ettin guards the passage to the new dungeon, which lies through the south wall. His chest holds a +3 ring of protection.
Cell Seven. Another great ghul guards a treasure chest with 200 gold pieces.
Cell Eight. Earth Elementals are trapped in this cell.
Hidden Chest. The treasure chest contains fifteen gems, a sundazzle shard, and 300 gold pieces.
Passage to the New Dungeon.
Your Rowboat. Step here to travel back to the ship.
Spine-throwing plants. They cannot be killed, so avoid them.
Cyclops. These have no choice but to fight you to the death.
Entrance to the Pillars. The treasure lies past this point.
Hall of Pillars. The chest is not as easily gained as it may appear.
Pile of Gold.

inhabitants.name: Ra’is Saris
inhabitants.description: The leader of the Ganlat deeply desires one of two things — either for her enemies’ leader, Ra’is Taraq, to be wiped from the ranks of the living, or for a peace treaty to end this violence. If you care to hear about her people’s troubles, and attempt to show her a way toward peace, she gladly accepts it. If you cannot do this or do not desire to, she will tell you how to enter the dungeon if you kill Ra’is Taraq.

inhabitants.name: Ra’is Taraq
inhabitants.description: Other than being of a different sex, the Razif’s leader is identical to Ra’is Saris.

locations.description: Your Rowboat. Step here to travel back to the ship.

The Well. This well is actually a secret entrance to the Al’Katraz dungeon. Once you have meditated at both shrines at Locations 14 and 18 and learned the magic words, approach the southern side of the well and speak them. The stairway then appears.
Empty House.
Ganlat Guard Post. These guards attack any strangers unless they are first scared and then reasoned with. You only have a short time to reason with them before they grow angry again.
Wounded. This makeshift hospital holds Ganlats who sound brave when you enter, but who are actually very scared.
Wounded and Unconscious. These sleeping Ganlat cannot be woken.
Ganlat Guard Post. If these guards see you, they attack on sight, believing that you are threatening the wounded.
Ra’is Saris. The leader rests here with her elite guards.
Empty House.
Razif Guard Post. These guards attack any strangers unless they are first scared and then reasoned with. You only have a short time to reason with them before they grow angry again.
Wounded and Unconscious Guards. These sleeping Razif cannot be woken.
Wounded Guards. This makeshift hospital holds Razif who sound brave when you enter, but who are actually very frightened. Speak to them for information about what has happened on the island.
More Wounded and Unconscious Guards. These sleeping Razif cannot be woken.
The Razif’s Shrine. This shrine cannot be entered without the blue key. Once inside, pray at the shrine for a magic word which can be used to uncover the dungeon’s stairs.
Ra’is Taraq. The leader rests here with his elite guards.
Flames. One of the leaders explains the importance of this flame.
Flames. One of the leaders explains the importance of this flame.
The Ganlat’s Shrine. This shrine cannot be entered without the red key. Once inside, pray at the shrine for a magic word which can be used to uncover the dungeon’s stairs.

inhabitants.name: Albino Ettin
inhabitants.description: Probably the strongest ettin in all of the Crowded Seas, this albino rules the western section of the dungeon. Before the mercenaries and sorcerers arrived, he controlled the entire complex. He now thirsts to throw the intruders out — or better yet, to kill each of them individually. Once the spiked doors at Locations 17 and 18 are opened and his argument is interrupted, he stalks off to crush Iskar.

inhabitants.name: Iskar
inhabitants.description: Mercenary leader; extremely strong fighter. Likely impossible to kill without help. His elite guards are also dangerous.

inhabitants.name: Kevric
inhabitants.description: Mage protecting the dungeon. Watches until it is clear you will rescue Tarik, then ambushes you with spells.

inhabitants.name: Tarik Al-Hazrad
inhabitants.description: Your brother, dying in his cell. After finding him, lead him out. His voice was taken by the Nameless Masters; it must be found so he can summon Muliban.

locations.description: Stairway to the Isle of Al'Katraz. Come and go as needed until Tarik is rescued.
Storage. Mercenaries' food supplies can be cut to shreds and left to rot.
Hallway.
Guard Post. Guard attacks to kill, as do all encountered guards.
Hallway.
Guard Post. Two guards.
Guard Post. One guard.
Vase Room. Vases can be smashed.
Empty Room. Western door is spiked shut and cannot be opened.
Treasure Room. Three baskets: one has two potions of healing and two potions of extra-healing; one has 175 gold pieces and a flame arrow shard; one has fifteen gems and a cone of cold shard.
Dining Area. Three off-duty guards attack.
Guard Post. Two guards. Door to Location 17 can be forced open by pushing until spikes give.
Sleeping Area. Guards' sleeping area.
Guard Post. Two guards guarding cell-block door.
Sleeping Area. Guards' sleeping area.

Iskar's Room. Iskar and elite guards are here. Entering before arranging help likely causes death. Gem on table drains prisoners' souls and can be broken with a scimitar swing. Prisoner register in northwest corner lists Tarik's name.
Hallway to the West. Leads to monster-infested western half. Open door to Location 18 like Location 12.
Ammuts. Two ammuts guard the area. One carries 231 gold pieces, the other twelve gems.
Hallway. Spiders roam here.
Hallway. More spiders.
Mummy's Room. Three mummies guard a chest with 149 gold pieces, one potion of extra-healing, and two potions of healing. They never leave the room.
Hallway. Fight bats and an ammut holding 160 gold pieces.
Mummies. Mummies attack fearlessly but do not pursue further into dungeon.
Hiding Place. Pillars form excellent hiding place on south edge of room.
Hallway. Blocks can be moved for quicker exit from Location 26.

Albino Ettin's Room. Ettin initially does not notice the door opening. After a few seconds he sees it and heads east to attack Iskar; do not let him see you. His chest contains twenty-five gems, 300 gold pieces, and a cone of cold shard. Follow battle noise to Iskar's room and wait outside until ready to join.

Kevric's Room. Key to jail cells is under bed with 500 gold pieces. Tureen shows a Nameless Master's face, allowing you to frighten him. Chest contains 500 gold pieces, potion of invulnerability, and potion of giant strength.
Hallway of Cells. Six locked cells.
Prisoner. Prisoners go free after cell is unlocked and they are spoken to.
Empty Cell. Prisoner has died.
Tarik's Cell. Tarik is nearly dead. Free him and lead him to safety; freeing other prisoners first is allowed.
Kevric's Trap. Kevric ambushes you and Tarik.

inhabitants.name: Muliban
inhabitants.description: The genie of your family is overjoyed to be reunited and under the control of his previous masters once again. He helps you continue your quest, but cannot directly threaten the Nameless Masters since he would again be coerced to serve them.

inhabitants.name: Tarik Al-Hazrad
inhabitants.description: Your brother waits impatiently in the hold for his voice to return. Once it does, he summons Muliban.

locations.description: Stairway to Ship’s Deck.
Storage Room.
Resting Area. Tarik will wait in this comforting room for the return of his voice. This is also where he summons Muliban once his voice is restored.
Your Rowboat. Step here to travel back to the ship.
Werehyenas roam freely about the island.
Empty Room.

Werehyena Room. A distraught werehyena guards a bottle in this room. Killing him frees him from his curse. A chest in the room holds a potion of extra-healing and twenty gems. Opening the bottle sends forth the trapped roar of a monster’s courage.
Pirate’s Room. A pirate guards a bottle containing the joy of a child. He also owns a chest with a potion of invulnerability, a potion of extra-healing, and 258 gold pieces.
Ettin’s Room. The ettin does not want to fight. His bottle holds a woman’s voice, and his chest holds 200 gold pieces and twenty gems.
Ammut’s Domain. Its chest is empty.
Pirate’s Room. The female pirate residing here will listen to reason. You can convince her to stand aside and let you free a man’s honor from the bottle.
Hidden Bottle. This bottle holds a heartbeat.
Brother’s Voice. Opening this bottle frees your brothers voice!

inhabitants.name: Gannat’tak
inhabitants.description: The Nameless Master retaining the greatest tie to human form is Gannat’tak. He wears the third necklace of seeing which allows entrance through every secret door on the level.

inhabitants.name: Zorth
inhabitants.description: Angry and upset, Zorth lives in a room full of wreckage — symbolic to him of his life. Although he is more human than Yanoir or Drackan, he sees in them what he will become and it strikes fear into his evil heart. However, this does not hinder him in fighting you.

locations.description: Entrance and Switches. To prevent traps at Location 2, pull and push each switch once.
Flame Jets and Arrows. If activated, dodge quickly. Arrows are poison-tipped and cause much more damage than normal.
Nas Nas Guards.
Nas Nas Room. Barracks. One nas nas wears weakest necklace of seeing, allowing wearer to see and enter some secret doors, all marked Location 5.
Secret Door — Level One. Visible when near while wearing any necklace of seeing. Cannot be passed until found.
Nas Nas Treasure. Chest contains 250 gold pieces, twenty gems, two potions of healing, and one potion of extra-healing.
Cyclops Guard.
Vases. One has 250 gold pieces, one has fifteen gems, one has potion of extra-healing.
Nameless Master's Bedroom. Zorth's room. He fights to the death.
Empty Hall.
Dining Room.
Cyclops Guard. Carries fifty gems.
Nameless Master's Bedroom. Yanoir's former room; unused because he drains prisoners in Unknown Plane. Dresser has notes; vase has fifteen gold pieces.
Cyclops' Room. Cyclops guard barracks. Renegade desert cyclops believe alliance with Nameless Masters will end persecution. One vase has 100 gold pieces and a cone of cold shard.
Nas Nas Patrols.
Halls. Approaching causes sorcerer at Location 18 to run out and cast a spell if he has any left.
Sorcerer's Room. Sorcerer guards live here. Useful information after killing both. Do not open spell books. Vases contain 600 gold pieces and a lightning bolt shard.

Hidden Sorcerer. Attacks from hiding when Location 16 is approached, then retreats where unreachable. Must be killed to get second necklace of seeing, which allows passage through Location 5 and 19 secret doors.
Secret Door — Level Two. Requires at least second necklace of seeing.
Arrow Trap. Open door quickly as arrows fire at your back.
Nameless Master's Bedroom. Gannat'tak's room. He wears third necklace of seeing. Search room for important information.
Hall.
Secret Door — Level Three. Requires most powerful necklace of seeing.
Treasure Chest. Contains fifty gems and a sunscorch shard.
Teleport to the Unknown Plane. Do not enter until finished with area; no return.
Sorcerer Guard.

inhabitants.name: Captain
inhabitants.description: One of the enchanted platforms at Location 9 is the same as the magic room of the ship, holding the gem which you were unable to completely destroy. Now, however, you are able to break it. After doing so, you are teleported back to the ship where the flesh and blood captain has some good news for you. Enjoy the brief trip and the brisk sea air, as soon you must return to the unknown region and complete your task.

inhabitants.name: Drackan
inhabitants.description: The mastermind behind the Nameless Master’s plot is also the least human. He has gained more power through the portals than his companions, and has lived in this plane day and night for years. He is currently filling the moonstone stolen from Zaratan with evil power. This additional strength will give him the ability to dominate even the most powerful genies.

inhabitants.name: Princess Kara
inhabitants.description: Your bride is trapped behind one of the mirrors at Location 12. She is in the body of a ghul.

inhabitants.name: Yanoir
inhabitants.description: This Nameless Master created the portals. He furiously attacks anyone found destroying them.

locations.description: Starting Platform.
Portal with Desert Cyclops.
Portal with Ogrima.
Portal with Mercenary.
Portal with Mummy. When mummy is defeated, a person appears too weak to aid you.
Portal with Great Ghul.
The Great Moonstone. Its goodness is being drained, but it still heals you.
Portal with Ettin. A human prisoner is inside the ettin's form.
Platform from Magic Room. Same platform as ship's hold portal. Break gem with sword to free captain; teleports you to ship.
Portal with Ammut.
Portal with Markeen. A Nameless Master appears when markeen is slain.
Portal with the Princess. Princess Kara is found here. Freashara secretly found a way to speak with her, so Kara may know of your quest.
Empty Platform.
Drackan. Final master and climactic battle. Use everything remaining. After killing him, Muliban appears and quest is completed.
